str.ljust() and bytes.ljust() or bytearray.ljust() can left-justify(left-align) the string and byte string set width respectively as shown below:

*Memos:

  • The 1st argument is width(Required-Type:int):
    • It decides the width of a string or byte string.
    • Padding is added to the right side of a string or byte string if width is longer than the length of the original string or byte string.
    • Don't use width=.
  • The 2nd argument is fillbyte(Optional-Defualt:" "-Type:str for str.ljust() or bytes/bytearray for bytes.ljust() and bytearray.ljust()):
    • It's the character to fill the padding of the string or byte string set width.
    • It must be one character.
    • Don't use fillbyte=.

<String>: 

v = "John Smith"

print('"' + v.ljust(20) + '"')
print('"' + v.ljust(20, " ") + '"')
# "John Smith          "
#            ↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑

print('"' + v.ljust(20, "?") + '"')
# "John Smith??????????"

print('"' + v.ljust(17, "?") + '"')
# "John Smith???????"

print('"' + v.ljust(14, "?") + '"')
# "John Smith????"

print('"' + v.ljust(13, "?") + '"')
# "John Smith???"

print('"' + v.ljust(12, "?") + '"')
# "John Smith??"

print('"' + v.ljust(11, "?") + '"')
# "John Smith?"

print('"' + v.ljust(10, "?") + '"')
print('"' + v.ljust(0, "?") + '"')
# "John Smith"

v = "   John Smith   "
   # ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print('"' + v.ljust(0, "?") + '"')
# "   John Smith   "
#  ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print('"' + v.ljust(20, "?") + '"')
# "   John Smith   ????"
#  ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

<Byte String(UTF-8)>:

bytes(): 

v = "John Smith".encode()
v = b"John Smith"

print(v.ljust(20))
print(v.ljust(20, " ".encode()))
print(v.ljust(20, b" "))
print(v.ljust(20, bytearray(" ".encode())))
# b'John Smith          '
#             ↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑

print(v.ljust(20, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(20, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(20, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'John Smith??????????'

print(v.ljust(17, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(17, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(17, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'John Smith???????'

print(v.ljust(14, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(14, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(14, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'John Smith????'

print(v.ljust(13, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(13, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(13, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'John Smith???'

print(v.ljust(12, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(12, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(12, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'John Smith??'

print(v.ljust(11, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(11, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(11, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'John Smith?'

print(v.ljust(10, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(10, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(10, bytearray("?".encode())))
print(v.ljust(0, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(0, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(0, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'John Smith'

v = "   John Smith   ".encode()
v = b"   John Smith   "
    # ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print(v.ljust(0, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(0, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(0, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'   John Smith   '
#   ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print(v.ljust(20, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(20, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(20, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'   John Smith   ????'
#   ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

bytearray(): 

v = bytearray("John Smith".encode())

print(v.ljust(20))
print(v.ljust(20, " ".encode()))
print(v.ljust(20, b" "))
print(v.ljust(20, bytearray(" ".encode())))
# bytearray(b'John Smith          ')
#                       ↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑↑

print(v.ljust(20, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(20, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(20, bytearray("?".encode())))
# bytearray(b'John Smith??????????')

print(v.ljust(17, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(17, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(17, bytearray("?".encode())))
# bytearray(b'John Smith???????')

print(v.ljust(14, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(14, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(14, bytearray("?".encode())))
# bytearray(b'John Smith????')

print(v.ljust(13, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(13, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(13, bytearray("?".encode())))
# bytearray(b'John Smith???')

print(v.ljust(12, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(12, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(12, bytearray("?".encode())))
# bytearray(b'John Smith??')

print(v.ljust(11, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(11, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(11, bytearray("?".encode())))
# bytearray(b'John Smith?')

print(v.ljust(10, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(10, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(10, bytearray("?".encode())))
print(v.ljust(0, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(0, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(0, bytearray("?".encode())))
# bytearray(b'John Smith')

v = bytearray("   John Smith   ".encode())
             # ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print(v.ljust(0, "?".encode()))
print(v.ljust(0, b"?"))
print(v.ljust(0, bytearray("?".encode())))
# bytearray(b'   John Smith   ')
#             ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print(v.ljust(20, "?".encode()))

# bytearray(b'   John Smith   ????')
#             ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

str.center() and bytes.center() or bytearray.center() can center the string and byte string set width respectively as shown below:

*Memos:

  • The 1st argument is width(Required-Type:int):
    • It decides the width of a string or byte string.
    • Padding is added to the left and/or right side of a string or byte string if width is longer than the length of the original string or byte string. *From which side of a string or byte string padding starts to be added is irregular if width is oddly longer than the length of the original string or byte string.
    • Don't use width=.
  • The 2nd argument is fillbyte(Optional-Defualt:" "-Type:str for str.center() or bytes/bytearray for bytes.center() and bytearray.center()):
    • It's the character to fill the padding of the string or byte string set width.
    • It must be one character.
    • Don't use fillbyte=.

<String>: 

v = "John Smith"

print('"' + v.center(20) + '"')
print('"' + v.center(20, " ") + '"')
# "     John Smith     "
#  ↑↑↑↑↑          ↑↑↑↑↑

print('"' + v.center(20, "?") + '"')
# "?????John Smith?????"

print('"' + v.center(17, "?") + '"')
# "????John Smith???"

print('"' + v.center(14, "?") + '"')
# "??John Smith??"

print('"' + v.center(13, "?") + '"')
# "??John Smith?"

print('"' + v.center(12, "?") + '"')
# "?John Smith?"

print('"' + v.center(11, "?") + '"')
# "?John Smith"

print('"' + v.center(10, "?") + '"')
print('"' + v.center(0, "?") + '"')
# "John Smith"

v = "   John Smith   "
   # ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print('"' + v.center(0, "?") + '"')
# "   John Smith   "
#  ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print('"' + v.center(20, "?") + '"')
# "??   John Smith   ??"
#    ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

<Byte String(UTF-8)>:

bytes(): 

v = "John Smith".encode()
v = b"John Smith"

print(v.center(20))
print(v.center(20, " ".encode()))
print(v.center(20, b" "))
print(v.center(20, bytearray(" ".encode())))
# b'     John Smith     '
#   ↑↑↑↑↑          ↑↑↑↑↑

print(v.center(20, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(20, b"?"))
print(v.center(20, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'?????John Smith?????'

print(v.center(17, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(17, b"?"))
print(v.center(17, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'????John Smith???'

print(v.center(14, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(14, b"?"))
print(v.center(14, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'??John Smith??'

print(v.center(13, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(13, b"?"))
print(v.center(13, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'??John Smith?'

print(v.center(12, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(12, b"?"))
print(v.center(12, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'?John Smith?'

print(v.center(11, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(11, b"?"))
print(v.center(11, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'?John Smith'

print(v.center(10, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(10, b"?"))
print(v.center(10, bytearray("?".encode())))
print(v.center(0, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(0, b"?"))
print(v.center(0, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'John Smith'

v = "   John Smith   ".encode()
v = b"   John Smith   "
    # ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print(v.center(0, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(0, b"?"))
print(v.center(0, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'   John Smith   '
#   ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

print(v.center(20, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(20, b"?"))
print(v.center(20, bytearray("?".encode())))
# b'??   John Smith   ??'
#     ↑↑↑          ↑↑↑

bytearray(): 

v = bytearray("John Smith".encode())

print(v.center(20))
print(v.center(20, " ".encode()))
print(v.center(20, b" "))
print(v.center(20, bytearray(" ".encode())))
# bytearray(b'     John Smith     ')
#             ↑↑↑↑↑          ↑↑↑↑↑

print(v.center(20, "?".encode()))
print(v.center(20, b"?"))
print(v.center(20, bytearray("?".encode())))

print(v.center(17, "?".encode()))
